name: Germany
intro: Germany's project culture is engineering-grade: automotive programmes with decade-long horizons, Industrie 4.0 factory transformations, and a Mittelstand digitising under competitive pressure. International certification has grown fast here as companies globalise their delivery organisations — PMP provides the common language with US and Asian partners, while Scrum credentials are standard across Germany's software and automotive-software teams. English-language instruction suits the international project environments where certified roles concentrate. Our live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, converting to morning-to-afternoon German hours with the evening free. You train alongside an international cohort in a compact two-day format. Afterwards we guide your exam application, provide full-length mock exams, and remain available for questions until you pass. German cohorts often include entire delivery teams certifying together before a transformation programme kicks off.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Germany?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Germany?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Germany.
